review_cadence: review before every candidate run, after every gate-policy transition, and before any external claim change

stages.label: Design-partner alpha
stages.intent: Controlled named-partner evaluation while exact-candidate qualification remains incomplete.
stages.claim_boundary: Controlled single-node alpha with a real Rust semantic kernel, limited to one visibility domain, trusted appenders, and explicitly supported deployment boundaries.

gates.title: The contained semantic boundary remains explicit
gates.requirement: Policy, proof identity, and append admission contracts must pass for the exact candidate before widening the alpha boundary.

gates.title: Boundary clients match the candidate kernel contract
gates.requirement: Rust, Go, and Python quality gates must pass from one candidate-bound workflow run.

stages.label: Commercial beta
stages.intent: A selected supported package, network, and schema boundary qualified from one immutable evidence bundle.
stages.claim_boundary: No commercial-beta claim is allowed until every non-waivable exact-candidate gate passes and the bundle verifies independently.

gates.title: Central semantic contracts are exact
gates.requirement: R1-R3 and full semantic acceptance pass without waiver.

gates.title: Readiness is computed from immutable exact-candidate evidence
gates.requirement: The official SHA/run/attempt-named bundle verifies offline and names the package attestation subject.

gates.title: Dependency and package supply chain is known
gates.requirement: Standard SBOM, vulnerability, license, code-scan, secret-scan, and package provenance subjects match the candidate package.

gates.title: Supported remote transport is authenticated and encrypted
gates.requirement: Non-loopback HTTP and remote Postgres use verified TLS with no automatic plaintext downgrade.

gates.title: Recovery, contention, performance, and soak limits are evidenced
gates.requirement: The exact package passes declared recovery, namespace contention, resource, performance, and soak policies.

stages.label: General availability
stages.intent: A separately governed support and distribution posture beyond the selected beta boundary.
stages.claim_boundary: GA requires independent support, incident, signed promotion, platform distribution, and distributed-truth decisions after beta.

gates.title: Support and incident response are contractual
gates.requirement: Support ownership, escalation, incident response, and signed release promotion are proven for the GA package.

gates.title: Supported platform distribution is verified
gates.requirement: Every advertised platform package installs, upgrades, rolls back, and verifies provenance.

gates.title: Distributed-truth claims remain separately gated
gates.requirement: No generalized cluster or consensus claim follows from the single-host partition prototype.
